rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are declared or visible in the current scope before use in AGREE contracts.
Use only valid AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts; avoid referencing undefined or inaccessible elements.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, validate that all referenced identifiers have matching declarations or are accessible in the current annex scope.
Ensure AGREE expressions use valid scalar types (int, real, bool) and align assumed types with actual expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers or types in AGREE contracts that are not declared or visible in the AADL model.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only assignments and equations there.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
Component type contracts and feature declarations
